More specifically, it belongs to the genus Rinorea, a group of flowering plants commonly known as violet family. intro: Rinorea brownii, also known as Brown's Violet, is a plant species in the Violaceae family, characterized by its small, fragrant flowers and edible leaves. The leaves of Rinorea brownii have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
